page: Microsoft Azure EC16eds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEDV5 server family. Powered by an Intel processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, ensuring secure and isolated virtualization.

For storage-intensive operations, the EC16eds_v5 includes a 474 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. This combination of high-capacity RAM and local storage provides a balanced resource density for workloads requiring fast temporary storage and large memory footprints. The absence of a GPU makes it a cost-efficient choice for non-graphical, memory-intensive workloads, avoiding the premium associated with hardware accelerators.

This server is designed for memory-intensive and database workloads. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications that require a high memory-to-vCPU ratio alongside local storage capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure EC16eds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEDV5 family, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture with an Intel processor, it provides 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and includes 474 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage. Lacking a GPU, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that prioritize memory capacity and local disk performance over graphical acceleration. It is highly suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
